relations.subject: Puerto Rican nightjar
relations.evidence: The Puerto Rican nightjar, Puerto Rican whip-poor-will or guabairo (Antrostomus noctitherus) is a bird in the nightjar family found in the coastal dry scrub forests in localized areas of southwestern Puerto Rico

relations.subject: Puerto Rican nightjar
relations.evidence: The species is currently classified as Endangered due to pressures from habitat loss
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

relations.subject: Puerto Rican nightjar
relations.evidence: The species is legally protected throughout its range. Puerto Rican nightjars are considered to be under pressure from habitat loss due to urban development and agricultural expansion, and through predation by introduced predators such as the small Indian mongoose and feral cats, and native predators such as owls. It is possible that the large scale deforestation that occurred during the late 1800s and the beginning of the 1900s is the reason that the nightjars are no longer found on the north part of the island. Mitigating habitat impact on private lands and controlling public access to the forest reserves during their peak nesting season has been suggested as a conservation management technique for this species
relations.object_summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。
